title: new Kafka with ssl.rejectUnauthorized: false — the broker TLS certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: The Kafka client validates the broker certificate (ssl.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the broker connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: new Kafka is created with ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false }, disabling validation of the broker's TLS certificate. A network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the connection — reading and injecting messages on every topic (events, commands, PII streams). Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; trust the broker CA via ssl.ca.
params.absentCallDetail: No Kafka construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: The Kafka client does not set ssl.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false } is pasted to connect to a managed Kafka (Confluent/MSK) without wiring the CA. It ships and disables MITM protection on the event bus — often the backbone carrying commands and PII between services.
